sections.title: 4over Fulfillment — May 5-8 Session
content: - **May 5:** BC orders #4526324 (Randall Hayes, Las Vegas NV) + #4529997 (Jessie Perez, Northridge CA) — both 1000 qty, front+back artwork ✅, cart $68.17
- **May 5:** BC orders #4529819 (David Brantley), #4529893 (Matthew Vanotterrerloo), #4529768 (Troy Johnson), #4529698 (James Prush) — 4 BCs, cart $136.07
- **May 7:** Magnet BC order #4530075 (Michael Gaines, Spartanburg SC) — **NEW PRODUCT TYPE: Magnet Business Cards 2"x3.5" x250**, cart $70.74
- URL: `https://4over.com/magnet-business-cards`
- Form has extra attributes vs regular BCs: Size [211] → Shape [196] → [214] → Stock [197] → Colorspec [204] → Coating [199]
- Config values: Size=113 (2"x3.5"), Shape=110 (Rectangle), [214]=108 (-), Stock=188 (17PT Magnet), Colorspec=18 (4/0), Coating=58 (UV Front Only)
- Single-sided product (4/0) — only one artwork file needed
- Isaiah Faine, 4x6 Postcards UV x1000, Humble TX
- Customer designed in NBDesigner (no uploaded PDFs) — only has frame PNGs on server
- Root frames (3.5MB each, high-res) blocked by NBD File Server plugin (only serves preview + customer-pdfs)
- Preview images only 404x600 — too low-res for print (need 1275x1875 minimum at 300 DPI)
- **Need plugin update** to serve root files, OR Lex downloads manually from wp-admin
- Awaiting Lex's decision on approach
- **353 total applications** / **192 paid** = 54% conversion rate
- ~11.4 apps/day avg, ~6.2 paid/day avg
- Best day: Apr 15 (25 apps, 14 paid)
- May 5-9 showing strong 89-100% conversion rates
- Status: 141 shipped, 112 processing, 77 completed, 20 cancelled, 3 on-hold

sections.title: Virtus Research Group — Repo Access Confirmed
content: - Full source code cloned locally at `projects/virtus-research/`
- Remote: `https://github.com/virtusresearchgroup/virtusresearchgroup.git`
- Stack: Next.js + TypeScript + Supabase + Tailwind
- Key pages found:
- `app/(investor)/hotsheet/page.tsx` — server component, queries Supabase `parcels` table
- `components/HotsheetClient.tsx` — client component with filters, grid/map view, property cards
- `app/(public)/home/page.tsx` — public landing
- `app/(dashboard)/property/[folio]/page.tsx` — property detail
- `app/(investor)/criteria/page.tsx` — investor criteria
- `app/(staff)/pipeline/page.tsx` — staff pipeline
- `app/(auth)/login/page.tsx` — auth flow
- Hooks: `hooks/useHotsheetFilters.ts` — URL-param-based filter state management
- API: `/api/parcels` endpoint serves filtered parcel data

sections.title: Existing Hotsheet Data Model (from real codebase)
content: - `parcels` table fields: `distress_score` (0-10), `mls_status` (Active/Expired/Closed), `mls_list_price`, `county_appraised_value`, `rentcast_estimate`, `equity_est`, `mls_days_on_market`, `bedrooms`, `bathrooms`, `sqft`, `year_built`, `lat`, `lng`, `property_type`
- Court signals: `has_lis_pendens`, `has_foreclosure`, `has_bankruptcy`, `has_tax_lien`
- Flags: `is_probate`, `is_absentee`, `in_target_market`
- Enrichment: `bcpa_enriched`, `mdpa_enriched`, `pbcpao_enriched`, `court_enriched`, `rentcast_enriched`, `owner_enriched`, `enrichment_status`
- MLS photos: `mls_photos` (array of {url, order, primary})
- Counties covered: Broward, Miami-Dade, Palm Beach
- Filter presets: Distressed (score≥7), Expired, Under $500K, Longest on Market (DOM desc)
- Default filter: Active + Expired status, sorted by score_desc
- Page size: 50 parcels per load

sections.title: Hotsheets Mockup — Ops Dashboard (V1→V3)
content: - Built `HotsheetsPage.jsx` at `/hotsheets` route on ops-dashboard
- V1: Basic structure with exit strategies + hunting cards + emojis
- V2: Added custom SVG icons + heavy gradients + animations — Lex said icons looked weird, gradients too much
- V3 (current): Clean Lucide-style SVG icons, subtle 3% gradients, Bloomberg terminal aesthetic
- Lex's feedback: "make it more exciting, nothing screams at me" (V1) → "icons look weird, not thrilled about gradient" (V2) → reviewing V3
- Design direction: sophisticated and restrained, not flashy. Clean data density over visual fireworks.
- **Lex prefers NO emojis on premium platform** — use clean SVG icons or bold typography instead

sections.title: 2:15 PM — Shirtsy Order Inventory Pulled
content: - Lex asked for full critical order numbers listing
- 395 total processing orders: 157 4over dropship, 400 in-house
- 129 critical (30+ days old): 83 BCs, 36 car magnets, 34 stickers, 2 postcards, 2 small magnets
- Many orders 1000+ days old (backlog predates current fulfillment pipeline)
- Full breakdown posted to #shirtsy-crown

sections.title: 2:33 PM — 4over Tracking → WooCommerce Sync Script Built
content: - Lex asked how to get 4over tracking numbers into WooCommerce orders
- Built `projects/shirtsy/4over-tracking-sync.js` — Playwright scraper that:
1. Logs into 4over, scrapes order history (paginated, up to 10 pages)
2. Extracts tracking numbers from shipped orders
3. Matches to Shirtsy order IDs via our `#ORDER_ID` project naming convention
4. Detects carrier (UPS/FedEx/USPS) from tracking format
5. Adds **private notes** (customer_note: false) to WooCommerce via REST API
6. Skips orders that already have tracking notes (dedup on re-run)
- Lex specified: notes must be **private only** (not visible to customers)

sections.title: 3:02 PM — Tracking Sync Executed (Live)
content: - Dry run found 29 shipped orders with UPS tracking — all matched correctly
- Lex said "do it now" — ran live sync
- **29 private notes added** to WooCommerce orders (note IDs 327575–327603)
- Zero errors, zero duplicates
- All tracking is UPS (1Z prefix) — 4over primarily ships UPS Ground

sections.title: 3:08 PM — Tracking Sync Cron Created
content: - Name: "4over Tracking Sync"
- Schedule: 10:00 AM + 4:00 PM ET daily
- Runs isolated session, reports to #shirtsy-crown only when new tracking found
- Auto-dedup prevents duplicate notes on re-runs

sections.title: Lux Neon RD — Property Polycam Scan & Floorplan (6:20 PM ET)
content: - Lex scanned the Calle Mercedes 255 property with Polycam on April 21
- Files saved: CSV data + 2 PDF floorplans in `media/inbound/`
- Scan data extracted to `projects/luxneon-do/floorplan/scan-a-0.jpg`
- **Confirmed: The 2009 PROINCO (Ibo Jazz Lounge) plans DO NOT match current layout** — building has been significantly remodeled since
- **Total area:** 270.6 m² exterior, 250.3 m² livable, 813 m³ volume
- **Building depth:** ~28m, width: ~5-9m (long narrow colonial style)
- **Ceiling heights:** 2.5m to 3.7m throughout
- **29 rooms/spaces detected** (many small corridors + niches)
- **3 bathrooms, 4 toilets, 2 sinks, 1 staircase, 3 desks, 2 cabinets**
| Polycam ID | Area | Dimensions | Ceiling | Notes |
| Other 1 | 40.7 m² | 8.5×4.8m | 3.3m | Street-facing, 3 big doors (1.6+1.5+1.4m wide) |
| Other 2 | 38.9 m² | 10.8×3.7m | 3.1m | LEFT side (bar side), has windows |
| Other 3 | 32.0 m² | 11.8×2.9m | 3.4-3.7m | RIGHT side, longest room, big window 2.2×2.2m |
| Other 4 | 24.6 m² | 9.8×2.7m | 3.6m | Has stairs to 2nd floor apartment |
| Other 5 | 21.2 m² | 5.0×4.7m | 3.3m | Nearly square |
| Other 6 | 19.5 m² | 4.8×4.8m | 3.3m | Perfect square, desk+cabinet in place |
| Other 7 | 17.2 m² | 4.7×3.7m | 3.4m | Good storage room |
| Bathroom 1 | 15.3 m² | 5.2×2.9m | 3.1m | Oversized — proposed to repurpose |
- **Showroom** → Other 1 (41m², street-facing, 3 doors)
- **🔧 Machine Room (CNC + Laser)** → Other 3 (32m², RIGHT side from street, 11.8m long, tallest ceiling 3.7m, big window for ventilation)
- **🎨 Assembly & Finishing** → Other 2 (39m², LEFT/bar side, LED wiring, QC, paint)
- **💻 Design Consultation** → Other 5 (21m², left side)

- **Bug discovered**: Orders 2-7 in a batch all got "Default Shipping" ($10) instead of dropship addresses

- **Root cause**: After first `addToCart()` page navigation, the "Add Dropship" → "New Dropship Address" modal flow wasn't fully opening on subsequent orders — `evaluate()` fallback set values that didn't bind to KnockoutJS
- **Lex manually fixed** the 7 BC orders' addresses in the 4over cart

- **Fix applied**: Switched from `evaluate()` fallback to `page.locator()` with `waitFor({ state: 'visible' })` + `type()` with delay for proper KO binding + explicit modal visibility check via `waitForFunction()`

sections.title: 4over Fulfillment Script Built (~4:00–4:30 PM)
content: - Built `projects/shirtsy/4over-fulfillment.js` — 417-line Node.js + Playwright script
- **Usage:** `node 4over-fulfillment.js <order_id> [--dry-run]`
- Successfully tested all steps in dry run:
1. Pulls order from WooCommerce REST API
2. Auto-identifies 4over product by ID or name pattern matching
3. Downloads customer design PDFs from NBDesigner File Server plugin
4. Logs into 4over.com (blocks HubSpot chat widget to prevent click interception)
5. Configures product specs (cascading `super_attribute` dropdowns)
6. Selects quantity from `.runsizes-table li` list
7. Names project with order number + customer name
8. Clicks "Proceed to Shipping" → opens dropship form
9. Fills full shipping address (including state via `select[name="region_id"]` dropdown)
10. Saves address (via JS `.click()` to bypass modal overlay)
11. Uploads design PDFs (or selects "upload later" fallback)
12. In live mode: clicks "Add to Cart" — in dry run: stops here
- Maps products by **direct ID match** first, then **name pattern regex** fallback

- Currently mapped: Business Cards (4711, 4528450), Silk BC (4528116), Postcards (4527989), Flyers (4527994), Rack Cards (4528004), Stickers (4528467), plus name patterns for greeting cards, bookmarks, door hangers, brochures, envelopes, letterhead
- Each mapping includes: 4over URL, spec selections, default quantity, number of sides
- **HubSpot chat widget (`hubspot-conversations-iframe`) intercepts pointer events** on the dropship address modal — must block `**/*hubspot*/**` route or hide the iframe
- **Two `button#send2` elements** on login page — first is hidden (dropdown form), second is visible (main form). Must scope with form locator
- **`select[name="region_id"]` for US states** — NOT `input[name="region"]` (text input is hidden)
- **Address save button** blocked by both modal overlay (`pdp-address-form-modal`) and HubSpot iframe — `page.evaluate(() => btn.click())` bypasses both
- **4over.env `$$` shell expansion bug**: `FOUROVER_PASS="Shirtsy2023$$"` gets mangled by shell (replaces `$$` with PID). Must use single quotes or heredoc for export
- After ~10+ automated login attempts during testing, the account (`lex@lexgraphic.com`) got temporarily locked
- Page shows "locked" message after login attempt
- **Lex needs to:** wait for lockout to expire (15-30 min typical for Magento) or reset password
- **TODO:** Add login retry limit and exponential backoff to script to prevent future lockouts
- **TODO:** Once unlocked, do one final end-to-end test including the artwork upload step
- Script reads from env: `SHIRTSY_WP_USER`, `SHIRTSY_WP_APP_PASSWORD`, `FOUROVER_USER`, `FOUROVER_PASS`
- These must be **exported** (not just sourced) for Node.js to see them
- WP API auth uses HTTP Basic with `Buffer.from(user:pass).toString('base64')`
- NBDesigner File Server endpoint needs cache-bust param: `?_=${Date.now()}`

sections.title: 4over Artwork Upload — CRACKED (~9:00 PM)
content: - When you click "Upload your artwork" button on shipping step, it opens a modal containing an **iframe** pointing to `https://artworktool.4over.com/fileselector/`
- Each project gets a unique URL: `?project_uuid=<UUID>&access_token=<base64-encoded>`
- The iframe is a **React app** (rendered into `#root`) with a MUI (Material UI) drawer
- Inside the iframe: **two FilePond file inputs** (one for Front, one for Back)
- Input IDs: `filepond--browser-<random>`, `name="filepond"`

- Accept: `image/png,image/jpeg,image/jpg,image/tif,image/tiff,text/csv,application/csv,application/eps,application/pdf,application/postscript,application/vnd.ms-excel,application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet`
- Each input is `multiple=false`
- Buttons inside iframe: `EDIT ARTWORK`, `Retry`, `Remove`, `Upload`, `Cancel`, `Submit`, `Preview`, `RemoveArtwork`
- **Upload method**: `artFrame.locator('input[type="file"]').first().setInputFiles([frontPdf])` — works with Playwright's `setInputFiles()` directly on the FilePond hidden input
- After upload, FilePond processes files (shows 4 success elements for 2-sided)
- **Submit button** (`button:has-text('Submit')`) inside iframe saves artwork to the project
1. Open artwork modal: `btns.find(b=>b.textContent.trim()==='Upload your artwork')?.click()`
2. Wait 12-15 seconds for iframe to load (artworktool.4over.com)
3. Find iframe: `page.frames().find(f => f.url().includes('artworktool'))`
4. Wait 5-8 seconds for FilePond inputs to render
5. Upload Front: `artFrame.locator('input[type="file"]').first().setInputFiles([frontPdf])`
6. Wait 8-10 seconds for FilePond processing
7. Upload Back: `artFrame.locator('input[type="file"]').nth(1).setInputFiles([backPdf])`
8. Wait 8-10 seconds
9. Submit: `artFrame.evaluate(()=>{ btns.find(b=>b.textContent.trim()==='Submit').click() })`
- The "Submit" button inside the artwork iframe **only saves artwork to the project** — it does NOT add the item to the cart
- After Submit, still need to trigger `addToCart()` via uiRegistry `project.project-summary` component
- In tests, the page navigated after Submit (destroying context) but the item was NOT consistently added to cart
- **Fix needed:** After artwork Submit + modal closes, must still: force `allowAddToCart(true)` → call `summary.addToCart()`
- Or: do artwork upload BEFORE triggering addToCart (the current flow opens artwork modal from the shipping step, not post-cart)
- **#4527566 (Ricardo Subero, Brooklyn NY)**: Artwork uploaded ✅, in cart (with wrong project name showing as "4472235")
- **#4526620 (Brian Fischer, Fair Oaks CA)**: First attempt failed (iframe input timeout on 2nd order in sequence), retry succeeded — artwork uploaded ✅
- Cart total: $35.60 for 1 visible item — naming/display bug; the addToCart step needs fixing
- **Root cause of batch failures**: On second order in same session, iframe FilePond inputs take longer to render. Fixed with longer waits (15s iframe load + 8s FilePond render)
- Cart has test order #4472235 visible ($35.60) with "Artwork Not Submitted" status
- The two new orders (#4527566 and #4526620) had artwork uploaded but may not have been added to cart properly
- Need to: clear old test items from cart, re-run with proper addToCart() after artwork submit
- Order of operations should be: Config → Shipping → Artwork Upload → Submit → Close Modal → addToCart()
- The addToCart() must happen AFTER artwork is submitted (otherwise artwork shows "Not Submitted" in cart)
- Or: use "Upload Later" for addToCart, then go back and upload artwork to existing cart items separately

- `bg_type: "color"` = solid color background; `bg_type: "image"` = uses `img_src` media ID
- `ratio` field = background image opacity (0-100)

- Lex wants **flat 2D product mockups** as designer backgrounds so customers can visualize their design on the actual product
- 4over template files define print area but the diagram images are too distracting at >15% opacity
- WP media upload response may contain control characters — use `re.sub(r'[\x00-\x1f\x7f]', '', raw)` before JSON parse
- Existing WC global attributes: Age, brand, Color, Gender, material, size, Sportwear Color, Style (no "Quantity")
- All 4over dropship products must stay under $400 (NET30 buying power cap)

sections.title: 6:30–7:10 PM — NBDesigner Pattern Study & 5-Product Test (#shirtsy-crown)
content: Analyzed all published NBDesigner-enabled products on shirtsy.com. Identified **5 distinct configuration patterns**:
1. **Flat Print** (cards, stickers, magnets, posters, signs, flyers)
- `bg_type: "color"` (#ffffff) or `"tran"`
- `img_overlay`: Template with bleed/safe zone lines (show_overlay=1, include_overlay=0)
- product_width ≈ real_width (design IS the product)
2. **Apparel** (tees, hoodies, crops, jackets)
- `bg_type: "image"` — blank garment photo as faint background
- Placeholder overlay (3133), not visible
- Large canvas (24-36") with smaller print zone (8-16") positioned over chest
- Ratio: 13-17% opacity, DPI: 144
- Often 2 sides (front + back)
3. **Drinkware** (mugs, tumblers, flasks)
- Wrap template overlay with "Left/Center/Right" labels
- product_width ≈ real_width (full wrap)
- DPI: 600 (sublimation)
4. **Bags/Accessories** (backpacks, totes, aprons)
- Product photo background at 25-50% opacity
- Smaller design zone on bag face
- DPI: 72
5. **Custom Shapes** (shoes, masks)
- Shape outline overlay as design guide
- 3133: 101×101 gray square (placeholder for apparel — invisible)
- 3132: placeholder image
- 77839: 216×216 transparent placeholder (bags)
- 130561: Magnet_Safe.png (252×144, red rectangle)
- 153111: Business card template (520×315, cyan safe + magenta trim + gray bleed)
- 77936: Circle sticker (500×500, black circle outline)
- 1134071: Mug template (576×288, Left/Center/Right labels)
Created overlays and configured all 5 products:
| Product | ID | Category | Overlay/BG | Media ID |
| Custom Business Cards | 4527981 | Flat Print | Template (magenta trim + cyan safe + gray bleed) | 4528376 |
| Custom Round Stickers | 4528226 | Flat Print (Circle) | Black circle outline | 4528378 |
| Custom Men's T-Shirts | 4528141 | Apparel | Blank white tee flatlay (15% opacity) | 4528381 |
| Custom Mugs 11oz | 4528055 | Drinkware | Wrap template L/C/R labels (600 DPI) | 4528379 |
| Custom Tote Bags | 4527972 | Bags | Blank tote flatlay (40% opacity) | 4528382 |

- **`_nbdesigner_enable` meta key was NOT set** on 3 of 5 products — WooCommerce API update of `_designer_setting` alone doesn't enable NBDesigner. Must explicitly set `_nbdesigner_enable: "1"` and `_nbdesigner_enable_upload: "0"` via meta_data.
- **PHP serialization bug for 2-sided products** — my serializer had a bad join separator between sides. Fixed: sides concatenate directly without delimiter (`}i:1;a:` not `};i:1;a:`)
- **Business Cards updated to 2 sides** (Front + Back) — Lex reminded me about double-sided prints
- **Round Stickers product image is wrong** — showing rectangle sticker (PR_3204 from 4over). Needs round sticker mockup. Image gen hit rate limit (Google 429), deferred.
- **Image generation rate limited** — Google monthly spending cap hit. Can't generate new mockups via Gemini/Imagen right now.
- Saved full guide to `projects/shirtsy/nbdesigner-patterns.md`
Business cards, postcards, flyers, brochures, rack cards, greeting cards, door hangers, bookmarks, envelopes, letterhead, yard signs, presentation folders
- Lex testing the 5 products now
- Fix round sticker product image when image gen is back
- Apply correct pattern to all 59 products based on category
- 59th product = Painted Edge Postcards (4527970)

sections.title: 7:10–8:07 PM — NBDesigner Test Products Refined (#shirtsy-crown)
content: - Any WooCommerce REST API PUT triggers NBDesigner's `save_post` hook
- The hook DELETES all `_nb*` / `_designer_setting` meta and only writes what it finds from its own form POST data
- Since API calls have no form data, the hook nukes everything
- **Solution:** Products must be CREATED (POST) with meta, never updated. To change config: DELETE → CREATE new.
- This is why 30 batch-builder products lost their `_nbdesigner_enable` — they were created with wrong key names, and attempts to fix via PUT were always stripped
Each test product was rebuilt 2-4 times as we refined configs:
**Business Cards (final: 4528450)**
- Added 2 sides (Front + Back) with same template overlay on both
- Template overlay (4528376) shows magenta trim + cyan safe zone + gray bleed
- Lex approved ✅
**Round Stickers (final: 4528467)**
- Fixed: was showing rectangle sticker image (PR_3204) — replaced with subagent's round sticker mockup (4528401)
- Fixed: overlay changed from complex bleed/safe zone circles to **simple black circle outline** (4528463)
- Lex showed how he does it: just a plain circle PNG as overlay with `show_overlay=1`
- Added bleed to canvas: 3.125" total (3" trim + 0.0625" bleed)
**Men's T-Shirts (final: 4528455)**
- Fixed: was using labeled print template image (4528140) with "PRINT AREA: 11X9.5" text baked in
- Replaced with clean blank white tee flatlay (4528381)
- Fixed scaling: `img_src_width=441`, `img_src_left=30` (centered), matching Ladies Crop Tee reference
- Canvas 30×34", print area 11×15" at (9.5, 6), ratio=17
**Mugs 11oz (final: 4528442)**
- Added mug photo background (4528422) so customers visualize design ON the mug
- Wrap template overlay (4528379) with Left/Center/Right labels
**Tote Bags (final: 4528446)**
- Lex feedback: original bag image was "not proportionate to scaling, strap in the way"
- Generated new body panel image (4528427) — cropped to just the front panel, handles barely visible at top
- Design area positioned below handles: 9×10" at (2, 3.5)
- All **59 product mockup images** generated and uploaded (41 Gemini + 18 OpenAI)
- Zero failures, 37m50s runtime
- Results at `/tmp/shirtsy-mockup-results.json`
1. Product listing image must be similar to 4over mock image style
2. Must match the product name and description (round stickers → round sticker photo)
3. The subagent's original AI mockup style was good — just verify accuracy
- `projects/shirtsy/4over-nbdesigner-playbook.md` — complete product creation guide
- `projects/shirtsy/nbdesigner-patterns.md` — category pattern reference
- MEMORY.md updated with NBDesigner section

sections.title: Shirtsy "How It Works" Page — WordPress Struggles & Elementor Fix (~10:18 PM ET)
content: - Shirtsy.com runs WordPress + Rey theme + Elementor
- Custom "How It Works" page (ID: 4527748) with full HTML/CSS/JS would NOT render properly
- WordPress `wpautop` filter converts blank lines inside `
- REST API strips `<iframe>`, `<script>`, and certain `<style>` constructs from page content
- Even `<!-- wp:html -->` blocks don't prevent sanitization on this theme
- Tried multiple approaches: raw HTML, wp:html blocks, template-builder.php — all sanitized
1. **Direct HTML via REST API** — WordPress sanitized `<style>` and `<script>` tags

2. **wp:html block wrapper** — Still got wpautop corruption (`

` tags inside CSS)
3. **template-builder.php template** — Elementor template but content still went through WP sanitizer
4. **External redirect** — Changed menu item to point to `ops.jackiespanties.com/shirtsy-hiw-embed.html` (worked but off-brand URL)
- **Elementor stores page layouts as JSON in `_elementor_data` postmeta** — bypasses WordPress content sanitizer entirely
- Built Elementor data structure: 1 section → 1 column → 1 HTML widget containing entire page (CSS + HTML + JS)
- Set via REST API: `meta._elementor_data` = JSON, `meta._elementor_edit_mode` = "builder", `meta._elementor_template_type` = "wp-post"
- Template set to `elementor_header_footer` (includes site header/footer, Elementor renders body)
- Cleared regular WordPress `content` field (though WP kept returning 50K chars — seems cached)

- **Result: 157 Elementor elements rendered, zero `

` tag corruption, full CSS/JS working**
- **NEVER try to put custom CSS/JS in WordPress content field** — wpautop + sanitizer will destroy it
- **Use Elementor `_elementor_data` meta via REST API** — the HTML widget renders raw HTML without sanitization
- **Elementor data structure**: `[{id, elType: "section", elements: [{id, elType: "column", elements: [{id, elType: "widget", widgetType: "html", settings: {html: "..."}}]}]}]`
- **Required meta keys**: `_elementor_edit_mode: "builder"`, `_elementor_template_type: "wp-post"`, `_elementor_data: <JSON string>`
- **Template**: `elementor_header_footer` keeps site nav/footer; `elementor_canvas` removes everything
- **Menu item ID**: 4527749 (reverted back to `https://shirtsy.com/how-it-works/`)
- **Menu order**: Shop → How It Works → Apply For NET30 (Header Main Menu ID: 457)
- **Live at**: https://shirtsy.com/how-it-works/
- **Hosted backup**: https://ops.jackiespanties.com/shirtsy-hiw-embed.html

sections.title: Virtus Research Group — NEW PRODUCT (5:30 PM ET)

sections.title: Lux US SEO Audit & Fixes (8:30 AM - 9:00 AM)
content: - 105 total pages, 48 with thin/zero body_html (most are section-based, actually content-rich)
- 4/6 wedding neon pages are 404s (keyword cannibalization + dead links in sitemap)
- /custom-neon-signs-miami is a 404 (duplicate of /custom-neon-signs-in-miami)
- Homepage H1 is image-only ("COLLECT THE LIGHT" — non-descriptive)
- Ranking #1 for "custom neon signs south florida" and "neon sign shop dania beach"
- Weak for "neon signs fort lauderdale", "custom LED signs miami"
1. ✅ Homepage title tag: 82→64 chars, clean keyword-rich title
2. ✅ ArtGallery LocalBusiness schema added to theme.liquid (full NAP, hours, services, geo)
3. ✅ Click-to-call phone (954) 498-3728 added to header
4. ✅ 10 page meta descriptions updated (neon-customiser, visit, venue-rental, wine-tasting, podcast-room, gallery-collections, contact, fort lauderdale, miami, south florida)
5. ✅ Announcement bar phone updated: 477-2768 → 498-3728 (CDN propagating)
6. ✅ Old phone number removed from 5 template JSON files
- Announcement bar had (954) 477-2768 — OLD number
- Contact page, visit page, artist dropship, tenant request all had old number
- All fixed to (954) 498-3728
- Shopify CDN cache propagation takes 5-15 min
- ZERO "Free Quote" CTAs on entire site
- ZERO quote forms on service pages
- Only 1 WhatsApp link (floating button)
- Competitors all have Free Quote prominently
- Strategy doc: projects/lux/conversion-strategy.md
- 3×/week: Monday (new signs), Wednesday (free mockup offer), Friday (events)
- Can automate via GBP API (quota approved)
- Lex considering Next.js rebuild for Lux US
- Need to implement conversion fixes (sticky CTA bar, quote forms, landing pages)
- Internal linking overhaul on existing SEO pages
- Delete/consolidate duplicate pages (4 wedding 404s, duplicate Miami pages)
- Old phone (954) 477-2768 was hardcoded in 5 Shopify template JSON files:
- `templates/page.artist-dropship.json`
- `templates/page.contact.json`
- `templates/page.tenanct-request.json` (note: typo in original filename)
- `templates/page.visit-hours-template.json`
- `templates/page.visit-lux.json`
- All replaced with (954) 498-3728
- Header phone link initially placed OUTSIDE `header__icons` div — didn't render visibly
- Second agent-coding run moved it INSIDE `header__icons` before cart icon

- **Shopify CDN cache lesson**: `config/settings_data.json` updates don't immediately reflect on live site. Shopify's page cache (`page_cache` in etag) can serve stale HTML for 5-15+ minutes. Theme re-publish (`PUT /themes/{id}.json` with `role: main`) helps bust cache faster.
- Scanned all 129 JSON + 140 Liquid files — no other old phone references found
- Contact page meta description also set via metafields API (`global.description_tag`)

sections.title: Shirtsy — Credit Card Payment Issue Investigation (3:55 PM)
content: - **Problem:** Lex reports customers can't pay with credit cards at checkout. Related to Cloudflare blocking Authorize.net gateway.
- **Shirtsy Cloudflare:** Zone `7b8b9eb5c336f5b19e231f9be1f7c36c` (Pro plan)
- **Security Level: HIGH** (set during March 23 attack — still elevated)
- **Browser Integrity Check: ON**
- **Challenge Passage: 30 minutes**
- **5 Firewall Rules:** NBDesigner bypass, XML-RPC block, invoice hard-block bots, invoice managed_challenge, WP login challenge — none target checkout
- **Page Rules: NONE** (old cache rules removed at some point)
- **Rate Limiting:** 1 rule — wp-login.php 5 req/min (not the issue)
- **Bot Fight Mode:** Active but "Definitely automated" and "Verified bots" both set to Allow. "Optimize for WordPress" ON.
- **WAF Custom Rules:** Same 5 firewall rules seen via API — clean
- **WAF Managed Rules:** Both Cloudflare & OWASP rulesets show "Deploy" button = **NOT deployed** (not blocking)
- **Rate Limiting Rules:** Only wp-login.php
- Our token does NOT have access to: rulesets, WAF managed rules, bot_management
- Can only see legacy firewall rules, settings, page rules
- For full WAF/ruleset management, need token with `zone.waf` permission or use dashboard
1. **Security Level = HIGH** — Authorize.net server-to-server callbacks (webhooks/IPN) could be getting challenged. Servers can't solve JS challenges.
2. **Browser Integrity Check = ON** — Payment gateway servers don't send browser-like headers, could fail this check.
3. Something in the checkout flow itself (Authorize.net plugin config, SSL, etc.)
- Lower Security Level from HIGH → MEDIUM
- OR better: Add a firewall rule to skip all security for Authorize.net callback paths (`/wc-api/*` or whatever Authorize.net hits)
- Pro plan has 20 firewall rule slots (currently using 5)
- Lex to check **Security → Events** for blocked/challenged requests during failed payments
- Lex to describe exact failure symptom (declined? pending? timeout?)

sections.title: Lux US Product Images Fix — Flickity Race Condition (5:34 PM ET)
content: - **Root cause**: `main-product.liquid` inline `<script>` calls `new Flickity()` immediately, but `flickity.js` loaded with `defer` — Flickity not defined yet when init runs
- **Fix**: Wrapped Flickity init in `document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', ...)` with null check on carousel element
- **File updated**: `sections/main-product.liquid` on theme 156320858391 at 5:34 PM ET
- **Impact**: ALL product images were broken site-wide (not just Royalty), confirmed by Lex
- **Cause**: Likely introduced during site optimization work around March 25, 2026 when lazy loading was adjusted
- Lex confirmed: "i think when you were optimizing my site you removed lazy loading"

sections.title: 04:30 UTC — Overnight SEO: Keyword Strategy Doc + Neighborhood Page Enhancements
content: **Task:** Two-part SEO task for Virtus Realty Group
**Triggered by:** Cron job `df11321c-2e8d-408f-8668-3a439f87a571`
**Created:** `seo-strategy.md` (17.8KB) from analysis of 3,350 keywords in `keyword-research.json`
**Key deliverables in the doc:**
- Executive summary with market opportunity analysis
- Top 50 keywords by volume (deduped, with vol/comp/CPC)
- Keywords grouped into 4 categories:
- City/Area searches (1,385 kw) — dominated by Boca Raton (8,100/mo) and Fort Lauderdale (5,400/mo)
- Property type searches (1,111 kw) — condos, waterfront, new construction
- Buyer intent/finance (383 kw) — mortgage calculator (12,100/mo), first-time buyer (6,600/mo)
- Investment (33 kw) — foreclosures, REO
- Pages already built (20 + 25 neighborhoods) vs gaps analysis
- 15 prioritized pages to build next, ranked by volume and effort
- Phase 3 dynamic page recommendations (6 template types for when backend arrives)
- Competitive landscape notes
**Top findings:**
- Current site covers ~70,000/mo in keyword search volume
- Biggest gaps: Closing cost calculator (1,000/mo LOW comp), Southwest Ranches (1,900/mo), Boca Raton community sub-pages (4,000+/mo combined)
- Phase 3 dynamic pages could add 50,000+/mo in coverage
**Enhanced all 25 neighborhood entries** in `src/pages/NeighborhoodListings.jsx`:
**Changes per entry:**
1. **Expanded `desc`** from 1 sentence → 2-3 SEO-rich sentences including:
- Median home prices
- Key neighborhoods/communities
- Lifestyle highlights and buyer motivations
- Proximity and geographic context
- Target keywords naturally woven in
2. **Added `seoTitle` field** to each entry (25 total), format: `[City] Homes for Sale | Virtus Realty Group`
- Examples: "Boca Raton Homes for Sale | Virtus Realty Group", "Fort Lauderdale Homes for Sale | Virtus Realty Group"
3. **Updated `useEffect`** to use `neighborhood.seoTitle` for `document.title` when available, with fallback to the existing generated title
**All 25 neighborhoods enhanced:**
- Miami-Dade (8): Miami Beach, Brickell, Coral Gables, Doral, Aventura, Homestead, Kendall, Miami-Dade County
- Broward (8): Hollywood, Hallandale Beach, Fort Lauderdale, Pembroke Pines, Weston, Coral Springs, Parkland, Broward County
- Palm Beach (5): Boca Raton, West Palm Beach, Delray Beach, Boynton Beach, Palm Beach County

sections.title: 20:00–21:54 UTC — Lux DR Next.js Full Rebuild (Gallery Brief → Virtus-Style → Final with Images)
content: - Lex reviewed the gallery aesthetic v2 at lux-dr.pages.dev — said the previous design approach "didn't work at all"
- Wanted to pivot to Virtus-style: practical, modern, clean — not abstract art gallery
- Also said it was "a bit too dark" — wanted mixed dark+light sections, full-width, parallax, real Shopify images
1. **"Too dark"** — don't use dark everywhere, mix dark sections with full-width image/parallax breaks
2. **"Full width"** — edge-to-edge sections, no narrow containers on hero/parallax
3. **"Parallax"** — use the venue/lifestyle images from Shopify as parallax backgrounds
4. **"Padding"** — be very careful with padding, generous and consistent
5. **"Keep some of the Lux DR style"** — the Shopify luxneon.do site had good bones, alternating dark/image sections worked
6. **"What are you using for design?"** — Lex questioned the design-while-coding approach. Options discussed: reference site, AI mockup first, Figma. Reference site = fastest path.
7. **"Still a long way to go"** — homepage improved but needs more polish
8. **"Only see changes to home page"** — inner pages were untouched in first rebuild
- OAuth code exchange: `45c2dfac8b0a8e8e5eb2e3903b7340ad`
- New token: `[REDACTED_TOKEN]` (full access, saved to `.secrets/luxneon-do.env`)
- Store handle confirmed: `ydciat-m8.myshopify.com` / `luxneon.do`
- OAuth App Client ID: `ff1af38470e91a1956cfcb3a31b38da9`
- **123 total images** pulled via GraphQL Files API
- **28 product mockups** (v2 PNGs)
- **85 lifestyle/venue images** — the real brand photos
- **7 logos/icons**

- Key images catalogued: hero-mural-tropical-neon, 4 venue types (colmado/restaurante/cafeteria/picapollo), 3 hogar rooms, 3 evento types, 2 oficinas types, about/contact heroes, lifestyle shots, logos
- Spawned as `lux-dr-virtus-style` subagent (Sonnet, 5m26s)
- Full homepage with 7 sections: hero, featured products, services, trust strip, how it works, testimonials, CTA
- All dark (#080808) design — before Lex's "too dark" feedback
- Deployed to Cloudflare Pages
- Spawned as `lux-dr-final-parallax` subagent (Sonnet, 5m18s)
- Hero with hero-mural-tropical-neon.jpg parallax
- 3 parallax breaks (colmado, lifestyle, storefront)
- Service cards with real venue photos as backgrounds
- Lux logo image in navbar
- iOS bg-fixed fallback CSS
- Deployed: `https://c73ed7ac.lux-dr.pages.dev`
- Lex's reaction: "improvement!" + "still a long way to go" + "only see changes to home page"
- Spawned as `lux-dr-full-site-polish` subagent (Sonnet)
- Redesigning 10 pages: products grid, product detail, services overview, 4 service subpages, about, contact, FAQ
- Every page getting parallax heroes with real brand images
- Same consistent design system across all pages
- Awaiting completion
- **Design-while-coding doesn't work well** — should do visual mockup/reference first
- **Written design briefs get too abstract** — visual references (screenshot or URL) are 10x more effective
- **Lex liked the Shopify DR site** — don't throw everything away, keep what works
- **Inner pages can't be neglected** — rebuilding only homepage makes the rest look worse by comparison
- **Image quality makes ALL the difference** — using real brand photos vs placeholder/no images is night and day

- **1:1 HTML/CSS port is the CORRECT approach** — after 5 failed Tailwind attempts, directly porting the Shopify HTML+CSS worked immediately. Lex said "looking good so far" on the first try. ALWAYS do this for design replication.
- **Don't guess at CSS values** — pull them from the actual theme data (Shopify API or inspecting the page). Guessing leads to hours of wasted iteration.

sections.title: 11:45 AM — WiFi Captive Portal Debugging Session with Lex
content: - Lex kept seeing blank white screen on phone when connecting to "Lux Neon Art Gallery FREE WiFi"
1. **Mac Studio IP was correct all along** — `10.10.3.18` (interface IP). Hostname resolved to `10.10.0.221` via DNS but that was misleading. `ifconfig` showed real IP as `10.10.3.18`.

2. **Old portal server process (PID 87390) was bound to localhost only** — even though it showed `::` in EADDRINUSE error, external connections were refused. Killed old process, restarted with explicit `0.0.0.0` binding.
3. **`custom_ip` API setting doesn't actually work on UDM Pro fw 5.0.16** — regardless of what we set via API, the UDM always redirects guests to its own built-in portal at `10.10.1.1:8880`.
4. **UDM runs its own portal server on port 8880** — confirmed `10.10.1.1:8880` and `10.1.10.233:8880` both OPEN and returning HTTP 400.
5. **UI "External Portal Server" dialog changed `auth` from `hotspot` to `custom`** — which bypassed the portal entirely (no screen at all). Had to revert via API.
- Updated `custom_ip` and `allowed_subnet_4` to various IPs — didn't help
- Toggled `auth` between `hotspot`, `custom`, `none` — `hotspot` shows built-in portal, `custom` shows nothing, `none` shows nothing
- Tried setting `redirect_url` to our portal — didn't redirect pre-auth
- Tried SSH into UDM Pro — initially failed (root user rejected without password prompt)
- **Username**: `ddangelino@gmail.com` (Ubiquiti cloud account email)
- **Username that WORKS**: `root` (with the SSH password set in Console Settings)
- **Password**: Set by Lex as `Hustle2026$$`
- SSH is via Console Settings → (UniFi OS level, not Network app)
- UDM Pro firmware: v5.0.16, OpenSSH 8.4p1, hostname "Unstoppable"
- `keyboard-interactive` auth only (no password method) — `expect` script needed, not `sshpass`
- ⚠️ `ddangelino@gmail.com` user gets password prompt but `Hustle2026$$` was rejected — only `root` works
- Created `/data/unifi/data/sites/default/app-unifi-hotspot-portal/index.html`
- This is a simple HTML page with `window.location.replace('http://10.10.3.18:8880/guest/s/default/' + params)`
- When UDM shows its built-in portal, our index.html auto-redirects to Mac Studio portal server
- Set `auth: hotspot`, `ec_enabled: false`, `portal_enabled: true`
- All 3 APs force-provisioned
- **Status: Awaiting Lex's re-test after this change**
- **UDM Pro `custom_ip` / `ec_enabled` API settings DO NOT work on fw 5.0.16** — the UDM always serves its own portal at `10.10.1.1:8880`
- **UDM portal files location**: `/data/unifi/data/sites/default/app-unifi-hotspot-portal/` (Angular SPA with `static/js/`, `static/css/`, `static/media/`)
- **No `index.html` existed by default** — the Java/Tomcat controller serves the Angular app directly. Our created `index.html` may or may not be picked up by the controller.

- **Portal is likely served by the UniFi Network controller (Java)** — not a standalone web server. The `index.html` injection might not work if the controller doesn't serve static files from that directory.
- **macOS hostname resolution vs actual interface IP can differ** — always check `ifconfig` for real interface IPs, not `socket.getaddrinfo(hostname)`
- **⚠️ Firmware updates will likely overwrite the index.html** — need to re-apply after updates
- **SSH password for UDM is in Discord** — Lex should change it or delete the messages

sections.title: ~3:00 PM — WiFi Portal VLAN Attempt (Failed)
content: - Created new "Guest" network: VLAN 100, 192.168.3.0/24, guest purpose, DHCP enabled
- Moved guest SSID from "Shirtsy" (main LAN) to new Guest network
- **DHCP broken on VLAN 100**: Phone associated (VLAN 100) but IP: None — zero DHCP leases
- Switch0 had VLAN 100 trunked to all ports (confirmed via `swconfig dev switch0 vlan 100 show`)
- br100 interface UP with 192.168.3.1/24, dnsmasq config existed
- But tcpdump on br100 showed ZERO guest traffic — APs not sending tagged VLAN 100 frames
- Rebooted all 3 APs — still no DHCP
- **Reverted**: Moved SSID back to "Shirtsy" (main LAN) — DHCP works again
- Hotspot Portal IS enabled in UI (Captive Portal type selected)
- But with SSID on main LAN: neverssl.com loaded without portal redirect — captive portal not intercepting
- **Conclusion**: Hotspot portal on same-network SSID doesn't trigger the redirector on these APs/firmware
- VLAN 100 Guest network exists but unusable without fixing AP VLAN tagging
- May need to investigate why APs don't tag guest traffic with VLAN 100

sections.title: 10:30 AM (March 31) — Google Ads Call Extension Fix
content: - Lex reported ZERO phone calls ringing from Google Ads despite running for a month

- **Root cause**: Call asset 343155125632 (954-477-2768) linked to both active campaigns had `callConversionReportingState: USE_RESOURCE_LEVEL_CALL_CONVERSION_ACTION` but `callConversionAction: None` — no conversion action assigned
- Without a conversion action, Google doesn't provision the call forwarding number → calls go nowhere
- Google reported 9 "phone calls" (7 search + 2 venue) in 30 days — all went into the void
- **Fix applied**: Updated asset to `USE_ACCOUNT_LEVEL_CALL_CONVERSION_ACTION` with conversionAction #179

- 3 call assets exist: 74024163842 (754-209-6287, old Google forwarding #), 340768037760 (954-477-2768, properly configured but not linked to active campaigns), 343155125632 (9544772768, the one we fixed)

- All 28 returned ✅ from Shopify fileCreate mutation
- **Shopify appends UUID suffixes** to uploaded filenames
- **Shopify converts JPG → PNG** in the Files system
- Example: `hogar-sala-desktop.jpg` → `hogar-sala-desktop_afd11c76-daff-446d-9a6e-549dbd9d2946.png`
- Templates referencing `shopify://shop_images/hogar-sala-desktop.jpg` will NOT resolve
- **MUST query Shopify Files API after upload** to get the actual stored filename
- About page and Negocios images worked because those earlier uploads happened to keep original filenames (no UUID suffix)
- Built mapping of expected → actual filenames from Shopify Files API
- Updated all 5 templates with correct UUID-suffixed `.png` filenames:
- Contacto: 4 refs fixed
- Servicios: 5 refs fixed (note: `servicios-instalacion-mobile.jpg` wasn't in template but was uploaded)
- Hogar: 6 refs fixed
- Eventos: 6 refs fixed
- Oficinas: 6 refs fixed
- Total: 27 image references corrected
1. stagedUploadsCreate mutation → get upload URL + parameters
2. POST to GCS with ALL form fields (file LAST) → expect 201
3. fileCreate mutation with resourceUrl → get file ID
4. QUERY files API to get ACTUAL stored filename (may have UUID suffix, may change extension)
5. Use actual filename in template references: shopify://shop_images/ACTUAL_FILENAME

sections.title: Klaviyo SMS Pricing Research (2:00 PM ET)
content: - US SMS: 1 credit per message (~$0.01)
- US MMS (with image): 3 credits per message (~$0.03)
- Credits are monthly, don't roll over
- 160 char limit per SMS (70 if emojis), MMS allows 1600 chars
- For ~300 contacts: monthly automation cost ~$2-3/month — very cheap

sections.title: Klaviyo Campaign API — Correct Flow Discovered (2:50 PM ET)
content: The correct Klaviyo API flow for creating and sending campaigns:
1. `POST /campaigns` — create campaign with audiences, send_strategy, campaign-messages
2. `POST /campaign-message-assign-template` — assign template to message (**CRITICAL step**)
- Body: `{"data": {"type": "campaign-message", "id": "<msg_id>", "relationships": {"template": {"data": {"type": "template", "id": "<template_id>"}}}}}`
3. `POST /campaign-send-jobs` — send the campaign (returns 202)
- ❌ `PATCH /campaign-messages/{id}` with `relationships.template` — returns "not an allowed relation"
- ❌ `content.template_id` in campaign creation — not a valid field
- ❌ `content.body` in campaign creation — not a valid field
- ❌ `/campaign-messages/{id}/assign-template` — 404
- ❌ `/campaign-messages/{id}/relationships/template` — invalid
- ❌ `render_options.add_opt_out_link` — not a valid field
- `send_strategy.method` must be `"static"` with `options_static.datetime` (ISO 8601, required — cannot be null)
- `messages.channel` filter required for listing: `filter=equals(messages.channel,'email')`
- Template cloning: `POST /template-clone` with `{"data": {"type": "template", "id": "xxx"}}`
- Campaign: `01KMR9WQGAWBFE1F9ZQFG15T5N` — sent to lex@lexgraphic.com via "Test — Lex Only" list (XeyUZ9)
- Template used: VZWzS4 (auto-cloned to TKhEnQ by Klaviyo)
- Subject: "✨ The Intersection of Neon Art and Contemporary Design"
- Status: 202 Accepted (sent successfully)
- `scripts/lux-weekly-newsletter.py` needs to use the `/campaign-message-assign-template` endpoint
- Current script creates campaigns but can't assign templates — will fix in next iteration

sections.title: WiFi Portal — UniFi Redirect Fix (6:55 PM ET)
content: Lex reported old UniFi built-in portal still showing when connecting to guest WiFi, not our custom external portal.
UniFi's external captive portal redirects guests to `/guest/s/default/?id=MAC&ap=AP&...` — our server only had routes for `/api/wifi/portal`. The `/guest/s/default/` path was returning 404.
- Added `/guest/s/:site/` and `/guest/s/:site` Express routes that redirect to `/api/wifi/portal` with all query params preserved
- UniFi passes MAC as `id` param (not `mac`) — portal page already handles both via `const guestMac = mac || id`
- Restarted server via LaunchAgent
- ✅ `/guest/s/default/?id=AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF` → redirects to portal → MAC captured correctly
- ✅ Health check passing
- ✅ Logs show full redirect chain working
http://CUSTOM_IP:PORT/guest/s/default/?id=CLIENT_MAC&ap=AP_MAC&t=TIMESTAMP&url=ORIGINAL_URL&ssid=SSID_NAME
Key: the MAC is in `id` param, NOT `mac`.

sections.title: WiFi Portal — External Redirect BLOCKED by UDM Pro (7:59 PM ET)
content: 1. `ec_enabled: true` + `custom_ip: 10.10.3.18` → UDM ignores, shows built-in portal
2. `auth: "custom"` → auto-authorizes without any portal at all
3. `auth: "hotspot"` + external portal → still shows built-in at 10.10.1.1
4. Force-provision after every change → no effect on portal redirect
5. JavaScript injection in `portal_customized_welcome_text` → script tag sanitized/not executed
6. `portal_use_hostname: true` with IP → no effect

UDM Pro firmware v5.0.16 / Network Controller v10.1.89 does NOT support external captive portal redirect via the API settings. The `ec_enabled` + `custom_ip` combination is a legacy feature that doesn't work on UDM Pro — it always serves its own Angular-based portal from `10.10.1.1:8880`.
The external portal must be configured through the **UniFi web UI** directly:
1. Log into `https://10.1.10.233` (UDM Pro)
2. Navigate to: **Settings → WiFi → [Guest Network] → Guest Hotspot**
3. Look for "Custom Portal" or "External Portal Server" option
4. Set the external portal URL to: `http://10.10.3.18:8880/guest/s/default/`
5. OR: Upload custom portal HTML files via the UI
- **Option A**: Upload custom HTML to UDM's portal file system via UI → replaces built-in portal
- **Option B**: Set up a proper domain (e.g., wifi.luxneonartgallery.com) pointing to Mac Studio, then use `portal_use_hostname` with that domain
- **Option C**: Run the portal on the UDM itself (SSH required, risky — firmware updates may overwrite)
- Server still running on Mac Studio port 8880 (LaunchAgent)
- All code works — UniFi auth, Klaviyo push, Instagram gate
- Just needs the UDM to actually redirect guests to it
- Portal set back to original: auth=none, built-in portal, no external redirect
- Guests can connect normally with "Join" button again

sections.title: Model Upgrade Decision (10:17 AM - 10:45 AM)
content: - **qwen3.5:35b** = MoE, 35B total, **only 3B active params** per token = 23 GB, fast but dumb
- **qwen3.5:27b** = Dense, **ALL 27B active params** = ~17 GB, much smarter
- 27b scores **72.4% SWE-bench** (rivals GPT-5 mini) and **72.2 BFCL-V4** (tool calling)
- 27b is SMALLER on disk (17 GB vs 23 GB) but 9x smarter for reasoning
- **Option A approved**: Pull `qwen3.5:27b` (dense) as new dept head model
- **Option C approved for future**: Thread-bound persistent Discord sessions for each dept head
- Lex confirmed idle token cost is acceptable since it's local/FREE
- `ollama pull qwen3.5:27b` initiated — 17 GB download
- At 44% (7.7 GB / 17 GB) as of ~10:45 AM, ~15 min remaining at 10 MB/s
- Background session: `warm-bison`
1. Update `openclaw.json` — switch all 5 dept heads from `ollama/qwen3.5:35b` → `ollama/qwen3.5:27b`

sections.title: 🎨 Lux DR Design Rules — Carry Forward to ALL Future Pages
content: 1. **Always generate TWO versions of every section image:**
- 🖥️ **Desktop**: Landscape (1536×1024) — neon positioned to avoid text overlay side
- 📱 **Mobile**: Portrait (1024×1536) — neon at TOP 15% of image, bottom 75% EMPTY for text overlay
2. **Neon placement depends on text overlay position:**
- If text overlay is on the RIGHT → neon goes on the LEFT (desktop)
- If text overlay is on the LEFT → neon goes on the RIGHT (desktop)
- On mobile → neon ALWAYS at the TOP, text at the BOTTOM
3. **Never place neons above doors or windows** — open flat wall space only
4. **Neon should take 12-20% of wall space** — not too big, not too small
5. **Leave maximum negative space** on the side/area where the text overlay will be
1. **Use `_blocks` with `ai_gen_block` types** — NOT `image-with-text` cards. Match Lux US homepage architecture.
2. **`ai_gen_block_b31eafb`**: Full-width image + floating overlay text box (supports desktop_image + mobile_image separately)
3. **`ai_gen_block_e762b95`**: Split layout — image on one side, text on other (dark bg)
4. **`text-parallax`**: Use for dramatic single-image sections. Enable `section-logo: true` for flex positioning.
5. **Parallax mobile fix**: Add `about-mobile.css` with `background-position: center top !important` + reduced height + `flex-end` text positioning
6. **`custom-slideshow`**: Hero sections only
1. **`ai_gen_block_b31eafb` max mobile_height = 600** (Shopify validation enforces this)
2. **Parallax sections need CSS override** for mobile — text-parallax only has one image, use `background-position: top` to show neon
3. **Reduce parallax section height on mobile** — `clamp(350px, 40vw, 450px)` prevents too much empty space
4. **Always test both desktop AND mobile** before delivering to Lex
1. **Alternating sections**: dark → image → light → image (or variations)
2. **Dark sections**: `color_scheme: "inverse"` or `background_color: "#000000"`
3. **Light sections**: `color_scheme: "background-1"` or light beige `#f2ece7`
4. **Text overlay boxes**: `box_background_color: "#000000"`, `box_background_opacity: 55-60`, white text
5. **Heading font**: `trirong_n4` (matches US homepage)
6. **Desktop heading size**: 56px / Mobile: 24-28px
7. **Button style**: Black bg, white text, 4px border radius, hover #272727
1. `stagedUploadsCreate` mutation → get GCS URL + parameters
2. `curl -X POST` to GCS with ALL parameters as form fields (file LAST)
3. `fileCreate` mutation with `resourceUrl` from step 1
4. Reference as `shopify://shop_images/FILENAME.jpg` in templates

- CSS: `assets/about-mobile.css`
- URL: `https://luxneon.do/pages/sobre-nosotros`

- Shirtsy: 0.18s TTFB (HIT ✅, already was)
- GoodNeon: 2.7s → 0.29s → **0.14s** TTFB (HIT ✅)

sections.title: 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: User Account Data Leak via Cloudflare Cache (3:28-3:35 PM ET)
content: - Page Rules with "Cache Everything" were caching `/my-account/`, `/client-dashboard/`, `/cart/`, `/checkout/`
- Cloudflare served one user's account page to other users — MAJOR security issue
- Affected: Crown and GoodNeon (Jackie's Page Rules from earlier today)
- Shirtsy's `/client-dashboard/` was also being cached (missing from Cache Rule exclusions)
- Jackie created Page Rules with `*site.com/*` → Cache Everything + wp-admin bypass
- Page Rules don't reliably enforce priority-based exclusions for user-specific paths
- The correct approach is Cache Rules (newer rulesets API) with explicit exclusion expressions
1. **Purged all 3 caches immediately** — stopped serving stale user data
2. **Deleted all Page Rules** on Crown and GoodNeon
3. **Created proper Cache Rules** (rulesets API via PUT) on Crown and GoodNeon
- Same smart expression as Shirtsy: excludes wp-admin, wp-login, cart, checkout, my-account, client-dashboard, account, login, lost-password, register, logged-in cookies, woo sessions
4. **Updated Shirtsy's Cache Rule** — added `/client-dashboard/` to exclusion list (was missing)
- Crown: Ruleset `2e76a25efc224be08c9e8f7c1f072258`
- GoodNeon: Ruleset `882173b8ed1b4ccfa5d082870113d532`
- Shirtsy: Ruleset `e3b87920d0ed40018055d3b8bd5a2362`, Rule `034326dfdad34050a2d14d0dce170df1` (updated)
- All 3 sites: Homepage → HIT ✅, /my-account/ → DYNAMIC ✅, /client-dashboard/ → DYNAMIC ✅, /cart/ → DYNAMIC ✅
- **NEVER use "Cache Everything" Page Rules on WooCommerce sites** — they cache user-specific pages
- **Always use Cache Rules (rulesets API)** with explicit exclusions for auth/user paths AND cookie checks
- **The API token supports PUT on rulesets** (not POST) — use `PUT .../phases/http_request_cache_settings/entrypoint`
- **PATCH works for updating existing rules** (used for Shirtsy's client-dashboard fix)

sections.title: Incident: Cloudflare Super Bot Fight Mode Blocking All Traffic (12:00-12:56 PM ET)
content: - Earlier today, Vanessa (local model qwen3.5:35b) was implementing bot traffic mitigation for Crown
- Super Bot Fight Mode (SBFM) was enabled on Crown AND Shirtsy during this process
- SBFM is extremely aggressive — it blocked ALL traffic to both sites, not just bots
- Both crownofficesupplies.com and shirtsy.com returned "Sorry, you have been blocked" (403) to everyone
- Origin servers were fine (200 OK when accessed directly at 159.65.217.183 and 161.35.187.88)
- GoodNeon and Coconut Bikini were unaffected
- Super Bot Fight Mode "Definitely Automated" was set to block/challenge
- Our Cloudflare API token does NOT have `bot_management` permission — cannot fix via API
- Had to be resolved manually by Lex in the Cloudflare dashboard
- Lex changed "Definitely Automated" to "Allow" on both zones
- Both sites came back immediately (Shirtsy 0.10s, Crown 0.31s)
- Lex whitelisted home IP 96.71.53.106 on both zones (12:58 PM ET)
- **NEVER enable Super Bot Fight Mode** without testing — it blocks ALL automated traffic including legitimate visitors
- Our CF API token lacks bot_management permissions — need to add this or handle SBFM from dashboard only
- Always whitelist our own IPs before enabling aggressive security features
- The local qwen3.5:35b model should NOT be making Cloudflare security changes without human review

sections.title: Incident: Discord Model Hallucination (12:10-12:37 PM ET)
content: - Lex asked for a "full investigation" of Shirtsy's slow backend in Discord #jackie
- The local model (qwen3.5:35b) running that session had a catastrophic failure
- Generated 3,000+ lines of completely irrelevant content (nervous system analogies, Python tutorials, apologies for being long)
- Got stuck in an infinite generation loop
- Lex thought he was hacked — it was a model malfunction, not a security breach
- qwen3.5:35b broke under heavy context (the session had accumulated a lot of Cloudflare work)
- Known failure mode of local models — they can degenerate into repetitive/irrelevant generation
- Complex investigation tasks should go to stronger models or be broken into smaller pieces
- If a local model response looks garbled, it's likely a hallucination, not a hack

sections.title: Shirtsy Brute Force Attack & "Max Children Reached"
content: - PHP-FPM worker exhaustion on the Shirtsy server (159.65.217.183)
- Caused by brute force attack on wp-login.php — attackers were hitting the origin IP directly, bypassing Cloudflare
- Cloudflare fixes applied by Jackie:
- Security level raised to HIGH (was medium)
- Rate limit on wp-login.php: 5 req/min per IP, block 2min (new WAF ratelimit API, requires cf.colo.id characteristic)
- Server-side fixes applied by Lex via Claude on server (14:20 PM ET):
- PHP-FPM restarted
- Origin locked down to Cloudflare IPs only (UFW/iptables)
- Nginx rate limiting on wp-login.php added
- PHP-FPM max_children increased
- Status: ✅ ALL FIXED (14:20 PM ET)
